ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Re: Verification Of Bank Of America And Morgan Stanley Accounts
Off Calendar Aug-25-2025 Granted Per Written Order

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Re: Verification Of Bank Of America And Morgan Stanley Accounts
Off Calendar May-29-2025 Continued To Aug-25-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Re: Verification Of Bank Of America And Morgan Stanley Accounts
Off Calendar Jun-02-2025 Continued To Jul-14-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Re: Verification Of Bank Of America And Morgan Stanley Accounts
Off Calendar Mar-26-2025 Continued To Jun-02-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Barry Schneider for Andrea Smith, Petitioner (via Zoom).

Mr. Schneider reported that, following the Court's prior order, the large account at Wells Fargo was successfully transferred. However, Bank of America has refused to release statements for the decedent's accounts without a specific court order. Mr. Schneider confirmed that Morgan Stanley had already complied, and that the only remaining issue was with Bank of America. He also reported the discovery of two creditor's claims that had not been previously delivered, which he expects to have verified through statements within 60 days.

Mr. Schneider requested that the Court issue an order directing Bank of America to release bank statements for the decedent's designated accounts, particularly those covering the date of death (August 11, 2024) and the prior month. Mr. Schneider was instructed to submit a proposed order via email to the probate examiner.

The court continued the matter to June 2, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Feb-10-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Barry Schneider for and with Andrea Smith, Petitioner (via Zoom).

The court inquired whether there was any proof that the BofA and Morgan Stanley Accounts were held in the decedent's name. Mr. Schneider stated that he had prepared a revised order specifying that account transfers would be subject to verification and directs the banks to provide account statements confirming ownership without requiring formal letters of administration. Mr. Shneider requested immediate distribution of a Wells Fargo account ($267,000) since there were no disputes regarding ownership.

The court took the matter under submission with the intent to approve the transfer of the Wells Fargo Account. A hearing was set for March 26, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, as to the verification of the Bank of America and Morgan Stanley accounts.

Counsel was instructed to send their revised proposed order to the probate examiner for further review.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
